result:
The spec is fully implemented. I added the three missing operations to `CsvRow.calr` in Calor, the build is clean, and all behavioral checks pass.

What I implemented (`CsvRow.calr:5-62`):

- **`EscapeField`** — calls the existing `NeedsQuoting`; if quoting is needed it doubles internal quotes with `replace` and wraps the field in quotes, otherwise returns the field verbatim (empty string stays unquoted).
- **`JoinRow`** — escapes each field via `EscapeField` and joins with commas using a first-element flag; an empty list yields the empty string. Parameter type is `List<str>` to match the pinned `List<string>` surface.
- **`SplitRow`** — a single-pass scanner with an in-quotes flag: outside quotes, a comma ends the field and a quote enters quoted mode; inside quotes, a doubled quote contributes one literal quote (consuming two chars) and a lone quote exits quoted mode. The final field is always pushed, so the empty line parses to `[""]` and a trailing comma yields a trailing empty field. It declares the `mut` effect since it pushes to the result list.

Two Calor-specific things I hit along the way: bare boolean conditions like `(needs)` parse as unknown operators, so conditions are written as `(== needs BOOL:true)`; and `[str]` maps to `string[]`, so the list type must be spelled `List<str>`.

Verification used 3 of the 10 build cycles. `dotnet build` succeeds with 0 warnings/errors, and I exercised the compiled DLL from a throwaway runner in /tmp (deleted afterward; nothing was added to the project): round-trips of `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` over commas, quotes, doubled quotes, CR/LF content, empty and trailing-empty fields all reproduce the input exactly, plus the edge cases `JoinRow([]) == ""`, `SplitRow("") == [""]`, and verbatim/quoted `EscapeField` outputs — all passed.
